symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged uncontrolled bleeding after dental extraction (8h, failing pressure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged bleeding after a mucosal surgical challenge is a hallmark of a primary-haemostasis defect.
Compatible with simple oozing but does not explain the systemic history or the duration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lifelong easy, extensive ecchymoses after minimal trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucocutaneous bruising is a core feature of the vWD bleeding phenotype.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent prolonged childhood epistaxis and gum bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucosal bleeding at multiple sites over a lifetime supports an inherited primary-haemostasis disorder.
Menorrhagia is a common presenting feature of vWD in women and drives chronic iron deficiency.
NSAIDs impair platelet cyclo-oxygenase, compounding an existing bleeding tendency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness on standing with quantified blood loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic symptoms with active blood loss signal early haemodynamic compromise mandating emergency care.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of deep haemarthroses or muscle haematomas

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep-tissue bleeding is characteristic of factor deficiency; its absence favours a vWF/platelet-adhesion defect.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ecchymoses in various stages with complete absence of petechiae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A petechial pattern would suggest thrombocytopenia; large ecchymoses without petechiae favour a coagulation/adhesion defect.
Compensatory tachycardia is an early sign of blood loss — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers compromise from reported lightheadedness, not a measured pulse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced vWF antigen and ristocetin cofactor / vWF activity with normal platelet count

investigation_details.investigation_name: von Willebrand panel (vWF antigen, ristocetin cofactor / vWF activity, factor VIII) plus FBC and coagulation screen
investigation_details.expected_result: Reduced vWF antigen and reduced ristocetin cofactor / vWF activity; normal platelet count; anticipated iron deficiency on FBC
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The specific diagnosis cannot be confirmed and a future procedural bleeding plan cannot be made.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced vWF antigen/activity with normal platelets confirms vWD — a laboratory diagnosis unobtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency in-person assessment with FBC, coagulation screen and von Willebrand panel
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the degree of blood loss and the underlying bleeding disorder; must be obtained in person as part of emergency haemostatic management.
